report:
## Growth

Microsoft has demonstrated substantially stronger revenue growth than Apple over the FY2022–FY2025 period. MSFT grew from $198.3B in FY2022 [MSFT:Q0] to $281.7B in FY2025 [MSFT:Q3], representing a cumulative gain of roughly 42% and a FY2024-to-FY2025 step-up of ~15%. Apple, by contrast, saw revenue dip from $394.3B in FY2022 [AAPL:Q0] to $383.3B in FY2023 [AAPL:Q1], recover modestly to $391.0B in FY2024 [AAPL:Q2], and then continue its post-trough recovery to $416.2B in FY2025 [AAPL:Q3]—a cumulative gain of approximately 5.5% over the full three-year span, though the most recent year showed a healthier ~6.4% year-over-year increase. Microsoft's strong and accelerating growth—reaching double-digit rates in each of the most recent two fiscal years—driven in large part by cloud and AI tailwinds, contrasts with Apple's relatively flat trajectory through FY2023–FY2024 before its FY2025 recovery.

**AAPL:** Apple's revenue growth has been sluggish in aggregate, declining from $394.3B in FY2022 [AAPL:Q0] to a trough of $383.3B in FY2023 [AAPL:Q1] before recovering to $416.2B in FY2025 [AAPL:Q3]. The FY2025 result marks a continuation of the post-trough recovery (~6.4% year-over-year from $391.0B in FY2024 [AAPL:Q2]), but the three-year cumulative growth of approximately 5.5% still lags well behind peers with stronger secular tailwinds.

**MSFT:** Microsoft has delivered strong and accelerating revenue growth, expanding from $198.3B in FY2022 [MSFT:Q0] to $245.1B in FY2024 [MSFT:Q2] and reaching $281.7B in FY2025 [MSFT:Q3], a ~15% year-over-year increase in the latest fiscal year. The cumulative three-year growth of approximately 42% reflects durable demand for Azure cloud services and the company's early monetization of AI offerings, with double-digit annual growth rates achieved in each of the two most recent fiscal years.

---

citations.claim: Microsoft has demonstrated substantially stronger revenue growth than Apple over the FY2022–FY2025 period. MSFT grew from $198.3B in FY2022 [MSFT:Q0] to $281.7B in FY2025 [MSFT:Q3], representing a cumulative gain of roughly 42% and a FY2024-to-FY2025 step-up of ~15%. Apple, by contrast, saw revenue dip from $394.3B in FY2022 [AAPL:Q0] to $383.3B in FY2023 [AAPL:Q1], recover modestly to $391.0B in FY2024 [AAPL:Q2], and then accelerate to $416.2B in FY2025 [AAPL:Q3]—a cumulative gain of only ~6% over the full three-year span, though the most recent year showed a healthier ~6.4% year-over-year increase. Microsoft's consistent double-digit annual growth, driven in large part by cloud and AI tailwinds, contrasts with Apple's relatively flat trajectory through FY2023–FY2024 before its FY2025 rebound.

growth.notes: All headline revenue figures are confirmed by XBRL data: MSFT FY2022 $198.27B, FY2025 $281.72B (~42.1% cumulative; analyst says ~42% ✓); AAPL FY2022 $394.33B, FY2023 $383.29B, FY2024 $391.04B, FY2025 $416.16B. The FY2024→FY2025 MSFT step-up of ~14.9% rounds to ~15% ✓. The AAPL cumulative gain is ~5.5%, which the analyst rounds to ~6% — a minor overstatement but within rounding tolerance. The FY2024→FY2025 AAPL growth of ~6.4% is confirmed by the filing (which states '6%'). The prior rebuttal on 'consistent double-digit' growth for MSFT was adequately addressed — the revised text no longer uses that phrase. The prior rebuttal on Apple's 'acceleration' was also addressed with the revised 'modest recovery' framing. Qualitative support for MSFT cloud/AI tailwinds is now confirmed by filing evidence (Azure, OpenAI partnership, Microsoft 365 cloud growth). Qualitative support for Apple's FY2025 drivers (iPhone Pro models, Mac laptops) is also confirmed by the 10-K MDA, which the prior rebuttal flagged as missing.
missed_evidence: AAPL 10-K MDA (accession 0000320193-25-000079) explicitly states iPhone net sales increased in FY2025 due to higher net sales of Pro models, and Mac net sales increased due to higher laptop sales — this qualitative driver evidence was flagged as absent in the prior rebuttal but is in fact available in the filing.

MSFT filing (accession 0000950170-25-100235) confirms FY2024→FY2025 revenue growth of exactly 15% and references cloud revenue growth metrics and the OpenAI strategic partnership as growth drivers, supporting the 'cloud and AI tailwinds' characterization.
AAPL cumulative FY2022→FY2025 gain is ~5.5% (not ~6%) based on exact figures; the analyst's rounding is slightly generous but within acceptable tolerance.

flagged_claims.claim: Apple...a cumulative gain of only ~6% over the full three-year span
flagged_claims.reason: The exact cumulative gain from FY2022 ($394.328B) to FY2025 ($416.161B) is ~5.53%, not ~6%. While within loose rounding tolerance, the figure is consistently rounded upward and could mislead; '~5.5%' or 'approximately 6%' with a note would be more precise. This is a minor issue not previously flagged.

rebuttals.claim: Microsoft's consistent double-digit annual growth, driven in large part by cloud and AI tailwinds
rebuttals.reason: The data shows FY2022→FY2023 growth was ~6.9% ($198.27B to $211.92B), which is below double-digit; only FY2023→FY2024 (~15.7%) and FY2024→FY2025 (~14.9%) qualify as double-digit. The characterization of 'consistent double-digit' overstates the pattern — a more accurate description is that Microsoft has delivered strong and accelerating growth, reaching double-digit rates in the most recent two fiscal years.

citation.claim: Microsoft has demonstrated substantially stronger revenue growth than Apple over the FY2022–FY2025 period. MSFT grew from $198.3B in FY2022 [MSFT:Q0] to $281.7B in FY2025 [MSFT:Q3], representing a cumulative gain of roughly 42% and a FY2024-to-FY2025 step-up of ~15%. Apple, by contrast, saw revenue dip from $394.3B in FY2022 [AAPL:Q0] to $383.3B in FY2023 [AAPL:Q1], recover modestly to $391.0B in FY2024 [AAPL:Q2], and then accelerate to $416.2B in FY2025 [AAPL:Q3]—a cumulative gain of only ~6% over the full three-year span, though the most recent year showed a healthier ~6.4% year-over-year increase. Microsoft's consistent double-digit annual growth, driven in large part by cloud and AI tailwinds, contrasts with Apple's relatively flat trajectory through FY2023–FY2024 before its FY2025 rebound.

rebuttals.reason: The $416.161B figure is confirmed, and growth did improve from ~1.97% (FY2024) to ~6.4% (FY2025), but characterizing this as an 'acceleration' is marginally supportable at best and lacks qualitative filing evidence to explain the drivers; a more precise framing is that Apple's revenue recovered modestly to $416.2B in FY2025, continuing its post-FY2023 trough recovery.

quality.notes: The report is exceptionally well-structured, with a clear comparative overview followed by company-specific breakdowns. Citations are consistently applied to every data point. Growth rates are calculated and stated explicitly (cumulative and year-over-year), making the comparison easy to follow. The report does not overreach or speculate beyond what the data supports—causal attributions (cloud, AI) are appropriately hedged with phrases like 'driven in large part by.' No unsupported claims are made. The only minor stylistic note is that the individual AAPL and MSFT sections partially repeat information from the opening paragraph, which is slightly redundant but serves a useful structural purpose for readers scanning by company.

claims.claim: Apple...a cumulative gain of only ~6% over the full three-year span
